description: Fix Profile Picture Upload Hang - Resolve indefinite upload hang, add timeouts, and improve error feedback

userStories.title: Diagnose and fix the root cause of the upload hang
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to identify why the profile picture upload hangs and fix the underlying issue so uploads complete successfully.
acceptanceCriteria: Reproduce the hang locally or against beta by uploading a profile picture
Check API logs (pino output) for /users/me/profile-picture endpoint to identify where the request stalls
Verify Firebase Storage bucket configuration - confirm admin.storage().bucket() has a valid default bucket or explicit bucket name
Verify Firebase Admin SDK credentials include private_key + client_email (required for Storage operations)
If bucket/credentials are the issue: fix the configuration and verify uploads complete end-to-end
If sharp/anime-filter is the issue: identify the failing image type and fix the pipeline
Upload succeeds and returns profilePictureUrl within 10 seconds for a typical photo
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Root cause: missing storageBucket in initializeApp(). Fixed with env var + default, added error handling and logging, parallelized anime filter.

userStories.title: Add explicit Firebase Storage bucket configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Firebase Storage bucket to be explicitly configured so we get clear errors instead of silent hangs when credentials are wrong.
acceptanceCriteria: Update admin.storage().bucket() calls in profile picture routes to use explicit bucket name from FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET env var with sensible default
At API startup, attempt lightweight Firebase Storage connectivity check (e.g., bucket.exists()); log warning at warn level if it fails - do NOT block startup
Add FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET to API's environment configuration in Bicep templates (infra/) and deployment workflows
Document the new env var in API's README or CLAUDE.md
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created lib/storage.ts with getStorageBucket() helper + checkStorageConnectivity() startup check. Added FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET to Bicep, deploy workflow, and CLAUDE.md.

userStories.title: Add server-side processing timeout for anime filter
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the anime filter to fail cleanly if sharp processing takes too long, so the request doesn't hang the event loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Wrap animeFilter() call in upload route handler with Promise.race against a 15-second timeout
If timeout fires, respond with 500 { error: 'Image processing timed out. Please try a smaller image.' }
Log timeout event with pino at error level including UID and file size
Add unit test for timeout behavior (mock animeFilter to never resolve)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Wrapped animeFilter() with Promise.race(15s timeout). Timeout is configurable via ANIME_FILTER_TIMEOUT_MS env var (for testability). Added 2 unit tests: timeout behavior + generic error.

userStories.title: Add client-side upload timeout for web
userStories.description: As a web user, I want the upload to fail with a clear message if it takes too long, instead of hanging forever on 'Anime-fying your photo…'.
acceptanceCriteria: Add AbortController with 20-second timeout to apiUploadFile function in apps/web/src/lib/api.ts
When timeout fires, abort fetch and throw user-friendly error (e.g., 'Upload timed out. Please try again.')
Account page displays timeout error in existing uploadError state
uploading spinner is cleared when timeout fires
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added AbortController with 20-second timeout to apiUploadFile. On timeout, aborts fetch and throws user-friendly error. Supports optional external AbortSignal for caller cancellation.

userStories.title: Improve upload error feedback in web UI
userStories.description: As a web user, I want clear feedback if my upload fails — not just a generic error — so I know whether to retry, use a different image, or report a bug.
acceptanceCriteria: Display specific error messages for: timeout, file too large, invalid type, server error, and network failure
After 5 seconds of uploading, change status text from '✨ Anime-fying your photo…' to '✨ Still working… this can take a moment for large photos'
Add Cancel button that appears after 5 seconds, allowing users to abort the upload
On error, show Retry button alongside error message
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added dynamic status text (5s timer), Cancel button (uses AbortController signal), Retry button on errors. Specific error messages already handled by apiUploadFile from US-004.

userStories.title: Add client-side upload timeout for mobile
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the upload to fail with a clear message if it takes too long, instead of hanging forever.
acceptanceCriteria: Add AbortController with 20-second timeout to apiUploadFile function in apps/mobile/src/lib/api.ts
When timeout fires, abort fetch and throw user-friendly error
AccountScreen displays timeout error appropriately
uploading state is cleared when timeout fires
Verify React Native fetch supports AbortController in Expo 55+
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added AbortController with 20-second timeout to mobile apiUploadFile. On timeout, aborts fetch and throws user-friendly error. Supports optional external AbortSignal for caller cancellation (for US-007 Cancel button). RN 0.83+ has full AbortController support.

userStories.title: Improve upload error feedback in mobile UI
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want clear feedback if my upload fails with actionable next steps.
acceptanceCriteria: Display specific error messages for: timeout, file too large, invalid type, server error, and network failure
After 5 seconds of uploading, change status text to indicate longer processing time
Add Cancel button or affordance after 5 seconds
On error, show Retry button alongside error message
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added dynamic status text (5s timer), Cancel button (AbortController-based), inline error display replacing Alert dialogs, Retry button for re-uploading last asset. Mirrors web UI pattern from US-005.
